I INTRODUCTION 
 
I.1 Product name  
GLOBAL_OMI_CLIMATE-VARIABILITY_NINO34_Tzt_anom 
 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
The average inside the NINO 3.4 box (170°W-120°W, 5°S-5°N) of subsurface temperature over 
the period 1993-2023 minus the 1993-2014-time average is evaluated to obtain a profile time 
series “depth-time” plot of subsurface temperature anomaly, expressed in °C. 
A multi-product approach is used, meaning that the subsurface anomaly is computed for 4 3 
different global 3D temperature estimates and for their ensemble mean  (GREP). The figure 
only displays the ensemble mean results but the file contains all the members. 
 
I.3 CMEMS products used as input for the computations 
 
Product name (and Type) Documentation 
GLOBAL_MULTIYEAR_PHY_ENS_00
1_031 
QUID: CMEMS-GLO-QUID-001-031.pdf 
PUM: CMEMS-GLO-PUM-001-031.pdf (copernicus.eu) 
INSITU_GLO_TS_REP_OBSERVATIO
NS_013_001_b 
QUID: Foreword (copernicus.eu) 
PUM: CMEMS-INS-PUM-013-001-b.pdf (copernicus.eu) 
The 3 reanalyses of GREP (GLORYS, CGLORS, ORAS5) GLOBAL_MULTIYEAR_PHY_ENS_001_031 
use the NEMO ocean model with assimilation of in situ and satellite observations. 
They are evaluated against INS TAC in situ observations 
INSITU_GLO_TS_REP_OBSERVATIONS_013_001_B in the NINO3 and NINO4 areas. 
 
 CMEMS Quality Information Document for 
NINO3.4 Subsurface Temperature 
Issue : 3.0 
29.09.2024 
 
 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
5 
II SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION 
More details and full scientific evaluation can be found in the CMEMS Ocean State Report #2 
(Gasparin et al 2018). The nino3.4 subsurface temperature OMI  is based on the “multi -
product” approach as introduced in the second issue of the Ocean State Report (CMEMS 
OSR#2, 2018).  
The scientific validation of this OMI  relies on the study of the consistency in between the 4 
different estimates (standard deviatio n), and the analysis of the average departures of the 
reanalysis GLORYS2V4 from in situ temperature observations in the NINO3 and NINO4 boxes.  
 
 
Figure 1: Temperature anomalies, from the 1993-2014 annual mean, area-averaged in the Niño box 3.4 from the 
multi-product approach. The thick black line indicates the position of the isopycnal 1025 kg/m 3, and the dashed 
black line indicates its climatological position. Black dots mark th

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document evaluates the Ocean Monitoring Indicator for sub-surface temperature anomalies within the NINO3.4 box from 1993 to 2023, using a multi-product approach with ensemble mean results and individual reanalysis products.

product_line.name: Ocean Monitoring Indicator (OMI) for NINO3.4 Subsurface Temperature Anomaly
